_meta.purpose: Global and cross-Kann relationships used by the prompt system. These are not day topics by themselves; they are support layers that explain how a Kannbeschreibung is performed inside a scene.
_meta.ladder: global ability -> domain/place system -> role -> small scene -> speech act -> text/utterance -> lexical field -> grammar tool
notes: Keep these inspectable in JSON so the relationship logic does not drift into runner.py.
Teachers may paraphrase these relationships aloud, but should not lecture on the KB ids.
Students may name the task in English or meta-language in early rounds; the teacher should recast it as simple German and move back into the scene.

relationship_groups.name: A1 conversation support
relationship_groups.summary: Slow partner, repetition, pauses, short utterances, intonation, and repair are the normal operating system for A1 speech.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: Use slow, clear speech. If the student names the task in English or meta-language, accept it as orientation, restate it in simple German, and put the student back into the small scene.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: Do not penalize pauses, English repair, repetition requests, or short prefabricated utterances when the student is still oriented toward the current speech act.

relationship_groups.name: Social contact operators
relationship_groups.summary: Greetings, introductions, well-being, likes/dislikes, small requests, and thanks are entry/exit moves inside scenes, not full replacement scenes.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: Use these as warm opening or closing moves, then return to the core KB scene immediately.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: If a response only greets or thanks when the KB requires another speech act, mark it as drift unless it also performs the target act.

relationship_groups.name: Numbers/time as exchange currency
relationship_groups.summary: Numbers, prices, times, dates, quantities, and short notes are portable information types across hotel, course, shop, transport, workplace, and appointment scenes.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: When the KB contains time/price/date/quantity information, make the grammar serve that exchange: 'um', 'am', 'von...bis', 'bis', numbers, and short note form.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: Treat approximate but intelligible numbers/times as communicatively successful at A1.

relationship_groups.name: Text/list reading
relationship_groups.summary: Short text, familiar topic, visual support, internationalisms, list-like structure, and repeated reading bridge directly into K163 and K176.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: Start from one visible item in the text/list. Do not ask students to summarize the whole text; ask what one item means and how to pass it to another person.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: Reward extracting one relevant information item. Do not require full-text comprehension.

relationship_groups.name: Micro-writing
relationship_groups.summary: Short social texts, chat, personal details, simple notes, and connectors like 'und/oder/und dann' connect forms, notes, e-mails, and everyday written exchange.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: Keep written tasks very small: one note, one form field, one sentence, or one connector.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: Grade whether the short written purpose is achieved before grading form or completeness.

relationship_groups.name: Oral production frame
relationship_groups.summary: A1 spoken output is short, unconnected, prefabricated, built from simple words, and often linked only with 'und/oder/und dann'.
relationship_groups.teacher_guidance: Accept fragments and prefabricated chunks when they perform the scene. Model a better phrase only after the student has attempted the act.
relationship_groups.grader_guidance: Do not require fluent connected speech; reward the right speech act in short chunks.
